data.narrative:
Radian Aerospace is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Richard Humphrey, Livingston Holder, Jeff Feige, and Curtis Gifford, having raised $27.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though the private set lacks sufficient same-stage clustering for robust median-based triangulation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using $28M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied value of $69M. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 42.9x across the 6-ticker sector set) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which is standard for private companies. Peer-median analysis was precluded by insufficient narrow-stage cohort depth—while 25 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 met adjacent-stage and similar-tier filters simultaneously.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $58M (low) to $79M (high), centered on $69M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single methodological leg and the absence of revenue data for cross-check validation. Key unknowns include product development stage, capital efficiency metrics, and near-term funding milestones that would anchor subsequent rounds.
